name: Guadalix de la Sierra y el canal del Atazar — Sierra de Guadarrama
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Guadalix De La Sierra are set within the Sierra de Guadarrama, offering varied landscapes for cycling. The region features a mix of mountains, valleys, and the Guadalix River, which carves a canyon. Riders can expect diverse terrain, including singletracks, and routes that traverse areas with reservoirs like Pedrezuela and Santillana, alongside forests with rich vegetation. Best mountain bike trails around Guadalix De La Sierra The most popular mountain bike trail is Guadalix de la Sierra y el canal del Atazar — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Atazar canal and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Zone of Gentleness – Stream crossing loop from Guadalix de la Sierra, a difficult 25.4 miles (40.9 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and stream crossings. Local mountain bikers also love the Dehesa de Navalvillar – View of the Pedrezuela Reservoir loop from El Vellón parking, a 32.0 miles (51.5 km) trail leading through the Dehesa de Navalvillar with views of the Pedrezuela Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Guadalix De La Sierra is defined by the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, the Guadalix River canyon, and the Pedrezuela Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes. The routes in Guadalix De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Guadalix De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Guadalix De La Sierra?
A: There are over 45 mountain bike trails around Guadalix De La Sierra, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Guadalix De La Sierra?
A: Yes, Guadalix De La Sierra offers 8 easy mountain bike trails. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38280356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Pedrezuela Reservoir – Pedrezuela Reservoir loop from Guadalix de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that is less demanding than some of the more difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Guadalix De La Sierra?
A: The terrain in Guadalix De La Sierra is varied, ranging from singletracks to wider paths, often traversing mountains, valleys, and areas around reservoirs. You can expect a mix of natural surfaces, including dirt, gravel, and some rocky sections, typical of the Sierra de Guadarrama. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33839036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zone of Gentleness – Stream crossing loop from Guadalix de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, feature stream crossings and significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Guadalix De La Sierra has 7 difficult mountain bike trails designed for experienced riders seeking a challenge. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38266505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Blas Hermitage – La Morcuera Pass loop from Guadalix de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1,000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass by notable natural features and landmarks. You can encounter the spectacular canyon carved by the Guadalix River, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the Pedrezuela Reservoir, as well as the surrounding mountains like Pico San Pedro. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19831967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Navalvillar – View of the Pedrezuela Reservoir loop from El Vellón parking&lt;/a&gt; specifically offers reservoir views.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Guadalix De La Sierra?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Guadalix De La Sierra are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38280356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Pedrezuela Reservoir – Pedrezuela Reservoir loop from Guadalix de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19831967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Navalvillar – View of the Pedrezuela Reservoir loop from El Vellón parking&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about riding in Guadalix De La Sierra?
A: The mountain bike trails around Guadalix De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful natural landscapes including the reservoirs and the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, and the options available for different skill levels.

Q: Is Guadalix De La Sierra accessible by public transport for mountain bikers?
A: Guadalix de la Sierra is located about 49 kilometers from Madrid. While specific public transport options for mountain bikers with bikes may vary, regional bus services connect the municipality to Madrid and surrounding towns. It's advisable to check current schedules and bike carriage policies with local transport providers.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Guadalix De La Sierra?
A: The region is suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter can bring colder temperatures, especially at higher elevations in the Sierra de Guadarrama, but many lower-lying trails remain rideable.

Q: Are there any mountain passes that can be reached by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Guadarrama, where Guadalix de la Sierra is located, features several mountain passes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38266505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Blas Hermitage – La Morcuera Pass loop from Guadalix de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; can take you towards significant ascents, offering access to passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1651353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia Pass&lt;/a&gt;, providing challenging climbs and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the area?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on established trails in the Sierra de Guadarrama. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, especially within natural parks or protected areas, and to stay on marked trails to minimize environmental impact.
tips: Good views of the reservoir, also known as El Vellón, with Guadalix de la Sierra in the background. The mountains that can be seen in the background are the ends of La Pedriza and the area of the Morcuera pass.

Road that circulates near what is called the Atazar Canal and that comes from the town of El Molar, passing through a set of hills that are not very high, but that in rainy seasons are green and beautiful!

Small town in Madrid near the Pedrezuela reservoir. Crossroads and roads of the Canal de Isabel II, it is the ideal place to have a snack mid-route, as well as to access the Atazar or Guadalix canals.

The Church of San Miguel Arcángel de Pedrezuela was built in the 16th century, it is rectangular in shape.

It has two naves separated by semicircular arches. It is in the late Gothic style. It has two fonts of holy water from the late 15th century.

https://todosobremadrid.com/que-hacer/iglesia-de-san-miguel-arcangel-de-pedrezuela/

Berlanga's film "Welcome, Mister Marshal" was shot in this town in 1953. There are two churches in the film, one of which is fake, but the other is the town's own gothic church, which is well worth a visit.

waypoints.name: Church of San Miguel Arcángel and Pedrezuela Fountain
Parish Church of San Juan Bautista, Guadalix de la Sierra
